title: Wire Security module to platform mastery/XP/streak tracking (retention layer)
description: RETENTION: useProgressStore has streakDays/totalXP/addXP()/updateStreak() — never called. useCrossModuleStore has moduleMastery — never updated. Wire: (1) Viewing all steps of a topic → updateModuleMastery('security', 'theory', 5) + addXP(10), (2) Completing a practice challenge → updateModuleMastery('security', 'practice', 10) + addXP(25), (3) Any interaction → updateStreak(). Dashboard radar chart comes alive. Source: Duolingo — streaks increase commitment by 60%. NOTE: Consolidates SEC-031/PLT-006.
acceptanceCriteria: Stepping through all steps of a Security topic increases theory mastery
Dashboard radar chart shows non-zero Security mastery after interaction
XP added for topic completion (10 XP per topic viewed)
Streak counter increments on daily Security module activity
Progress persists across sessions via localStorage

title: Add 'Topic Completed' celebration with streak display and next-topic suggestion
description: RETENTION: When user reaches last step of a step-through topic, show a celebration card: confetti animation + '✅ OAuth 2.0 Completed! Day 3 streak 🔥 +10 XP' + 'Next up: JWT Lifecycle →'. Combines completion signal + streak reinforcement + progression nudge. Source: Duolingo lesson completion screen. Frontend-only using existing notification store.
acceptanceCriteria: Celebration card appears when reaching last step of OAuth/DH/HTTPS topics
Shows streak count and XP earned
Suggests next related topic with clickable link
Confetti or pulse animation on celebration (respects prefers-reduced-motion)
Card is dismissible and non-blocking

title: Add spaced repetition review prompts ('Review DH — last seen 3 days ago')
description: RETENTION: Track when each Security topic was last viewed in localStorage. After 1 day, 3 days, 7 days, 14 days (SM-2 intervals), show a subtle 'Review' badge in the sidebar: '📝 Review: Diffie-Hellman (3 days ago)'. Clicking reopens the topic. Based on Ebbinghaus forgetting curve: 70% forgotten within 24h without review. Frontend-only.
acceptanceCriteria: localStorage tracks lastViewed timestamp per Security topic
Sidebar shows 'Review' badge on topics due for review
Review intervals follow SM-2 schedule (1d, 3d, 7d, 14d)
Clicking review badge opens the topic
Review badge disappears after topic is re-viewed

title: Add 'Share Visualization' button that copies URL with topic + step state
description: COMMUNITY/DISCOVERY: After PLT-001 (URL routing), add a 'Share' button to the Security canvas that copies a URL like `architex.dev/m/security/diffie-hellman?step=5`. The recipient opens the link and sees the exact same visualization state. Also generates an Open Graph preview image showing the visualization. Source: Figma's 'Copy link to this frame' pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Share button visible on every Security canvas
Copies URL encoding: module + topic + step index
Opening shared URL loads exact visualization state
Toast confirms 'Link copied!'
Works after PLT-001 URL routing is implemented

title: Add 'Export as PNG' button for sharing visualizations on social media
description: COMMUNITY/DISCOVERY: Add export button that captures the current canvas visualization as a PNG using html2canvas. Include topic title and Architex branding as watermark. Use case: CS students sharing DH paint analogy on Twitter, professors embedding AES matrix in slides. Frontend-only.
acceptanceCriteria: Export button renders on every Security canvas
Clicking generates PNG of the current visualization
PNG includes topic title and 'architex.dev' watermark
Image downloads automatically with descriptive filename
Works for all 11 Security topics

title: Add per-topic JSON-LD structured data (schema.org/LearningResource)
description: DISCOVERY: No structured data exists. Add JSON-LD for each Security topic using schema.org/LearningResource: name, description, educationalLevel, teaches, interactivityType ('active'), learningResourceType ('interactive visualization'). This enables AI-powered search (ChatGPT, Perplexity) and Google rich snippets. After PLT-001 gives each topic its own URL, add `<script type='application/ld+json'>` per topic page.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON-LD LearningResource schema on Security topic pages
Includes: name, description, teaches (topic concepts), educationalLevel
Valid per Google's structured data testing tool
At least 5 Security topics have structured data

title: Add per-topic Open Graph meta tags and dynamic OG image generation
description: DISCOVERY: layout.tsx has platform-level OG tags only. After PLT-001, each Security topic needs: `<title>OAuth 2.0 Visualization — Interactive Step-by-Step | Architex</title>`, `<meta property='og:image' content='/api/og?topic=oauth&module=security'>`. The /api/og endpoint already exists — extend it to accept topic parameters and generate topic-specific preview images showing a screenshot of the visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: Each Security topic URL has unique title and description meta tags
OG image shows a representative screenshot of the topic's visualization
Twitter card shows properly when sharing a Security topic URL
At least 5 topics have topic-specific OG images

title: Add 'What Would Eve See?' toggle for all crypto topics (DH, AES, HTTPS, Encryption)
description: INNOVATION: DH already has Eve's panel (SecurityModule.tsx:792-838) showing what an eavesdropper can/cannot see. Extend to ALL crypto topics: AES (Eve sees ciphertext but not plaintext or key), HTTPS (Eve sees encrypted packets but not content), Encryption Comparison (Eve sees network traffic but not session key). Toggle button 'Eve's View' grays out all private/secret data and highlights only public information. This powerfully teaches WHAT encryption actually protects. No competitor has this.
acceptanceCriteria: Eve toggle button on DH (enhance existing), AES, HTTPS, Encryption topics
Activating grays out: private keys, plaintext, session keys, decrypted data
Highlights: public keys, ciphertext, network-visible data
Eve panel explains WHY she can't derive the private data
Toggle is non-destructive — deactivating restores full view

title: Add CORS Error Translator — paste browser error, get human explanation + fix
description: INNOVATION: CORS errors are the #1 most-googled developer error. Build a 'CORS Doctor' tool: user pastes browser console error ('Access to XMLHttpRequest at ... from origin ... has been blocked by CORS policy: No Access-Control-Allow-Origin header'), tool parses it and explains: (1) what went wrong in plain English, (2) which CORS step failed (with link to CORS visualization), (3) exact fix (server-side header to add). No competitor has this. Frontend-only (regex parsing of error text).
acceptanceCriteria: Text input where user pastes CORS error message
Parser extracts: origin, target, failed check, missing header
Plain English explanation of why it failed
Specific fix: exact header to add on the server
Link to CORS visualization showing the failed step highlighted

title: Add AES sonification — hear the difference between encryption rounds (Web Audio API)
description: INNOVATION: Map AES round states to audio. SubBytes: each byte value maps to a pitch (0x00=low, 0xFF=high), played as a chord. ShiftRows: sliding tone sweep. MixColumns: harmonic blend. AddRoundKey: percussive XOR click. Round 1 sounds ordered (plaintext has patterns), round 10 sounds random (encrypted). User HEARS that encryption destroys patterns. No algorithm visualizer has done this. Web Audio API, frontend-only.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio toggle on AES visualization (off by default)
Each sub-step has a distinct audio character
Round 1 sounds different from round 10 (patterns → noise)
Volume control and mute button
Respects browser audio autoplay policies

title: Add 'Security Wrapped' — annual summary of what you learned (Spotify Wrapped style)
description: INNOVATION/RETENTION: At year-end (or on-demand), generate a beautiful shareable card: 'In 2026, you explored 9/11 Security topics, completed 47 steps, mastered DH in 3 attempts, your hardest topic was AES. You're in the top 15% of Security learners.' Shareable as PNG. Builds on existing progress/mastery stores. Source: Spotify Wrapped — 60M+ shares per year. Frontend-only (generates from localStorage data).
acceptanceCriteria: Accessible from dashboard or settings
Shows: topics explored, total steps, mastery scores, hardest topic
Beautiful card design matching Architex aesthetic
Exportable as PNG for social sharing
Works even without user account (localStorage data)

title: Add embeddable Security visualization widget for blog posts
description: DISCOVERY/COMMUNITY: Create an embed route `/embed/security/{topic}` that renders a minimal, self-contained visualization suitable for iframe embedding. Use case: blogger writes about OAuth, embeds `<iframe src='architex.dev/embed/security/oauth?step=5&theme=dark' width=600 height=400></iframe>`. Widget shows visualization + step controls, no sidebar/properties/activity bar. Include 'Powered by Architex' footer link.
acceptanceCriteria: /embed/security/{topic} route renders minimal visualization
No sidebar, activity bar, or properties panel — just canvas + step controls
Configurable via URL params: topic, step, theme (dark/light)
'Powered by Architex' footer links to full platform
Works in iframe with reasonable dimensions (min 400x300)

title: Add sitemap.xml with all Security topics for search engine indexing
description: DISCOVERY: No sitemap exists. After PLT-001 (URL routing), generate a sitemap.xml that includes all 13 Security topics: /m/security/oauth, /m/security/jwt, /m/security/aes, etc. Include lastmod dates and priority weights. Submit to Google Search Console. Also add robots.txt allowing crawler access.
acceptanceCriteria: sitemap.xml includes all Security topic URLs
Each entry has lastmod date and priority
robots.txt allows search engine crawling
Sitemap is auto-generated (not manually maintained)
